title: Supporting Artifacts & Digital Footprint
description: Build the infrastructure that makes your fake persona 'real': LinkedIn, email, domains, and more.

slides.title: The Digital Paper Trail 🌐
slides.content:
# If It's Not on Google, It Doesn't Exist

In 2026, the first thing a suspicious target does is **Google your name**.

**What They Expect to Find:**
- LinkedIn profile with a professional photo.
- Company website listing you as an employee.
- Maybe a Twitter/X account with industry-related posts.
- A few conference speaker bios or blog posts (optional, advanced).

**What Raises Red Flags:**
- **Zero online presence.** Everyone has *something*.
- **A brand new LinkedIn profile** with 2 connections.
- **A company website** that looks like a template from 1999.

**The Goal:** Create a **minimum viable digital footprint** that survives a **10-minute Google search**.

quizData.question: Why is a brand new LinkedIn profile with few connections a red flag?

options.text: Real professionals have established networks and activity history
It's against LinkedIn's terms of service

quizData.explanation: Correct. A profile with a join date of 'this month' and few connections screams 'burner account'.

slides.title: Building a Fake LinkedIn Profile 👔
slides.content:
# The Long Game

**Timeline:** You cannot create a convincing LinkedIn profile overnight. You need **aged accounts**.

**Method 1: Purchase Aged Accounts (Gray Area)**
- Some underground markets sell LinkedIn accounts created years ago.
- **Risk:** Account may be reclaimed or flagged. Violates LinkedIn ToS.

**Method 2: Grow It Organically (Best for Red Team)**
- **Start 3-6 months before the engagement.**
- **Step 1:** Create the account with your fake name and AI-generated photo.
- **Step 2:** Add **500+ connections** slowly. Use LinkedIn's 'People You May Know' and connect with **random professionals** in the same industry. Accept all incoming requests.
- **Step 3:** Post **generic industry content** once a week. (Use ChatGPT to write bland posts about 'The Future of Project Management').
- **Step 4:** Join **industry groups** and occasionally comment: *"Great insights!"*

**Result:** After 6 months, your profile looks like a **real, slightly boring professional**. It passes the 'sniff test'.

slides.title: Creating the Fake Company Website 🏢
slides.content:
# A Plausible Storefront

**Domain Registration:**
- Use **privacy-protected WHOIS** (standard with most registrars).
- Choose a **.com** or **.io** domain that sounds like a real consulting firm or tech company.
- Example: `Apex-Solutions.com` or `Crestview-Consulting.com`.

**Website Content:**
- Use a **static site generator** (Hugo, Jekyll) or a simple WordPress template.
- **Pages Needed:**
  - **Home:** Generic corporate mission statement.
  - **About Us:** Vague history ('Founded in 2018').
  - **Team:** List your fake persona as 'Senior Project Manager' with the AI photo.
  - **Contact:** A **contact form** (emails go to your burner email) and a **phone number** (burner VoIP).
- **SSL Certificate:** **Essential.** Use Let's Encrypt.

**The 'Under Construction' Trap:** A website that says 'Under Construction' or has placeholder text is **worse than no website**. It screams 'fake'.

quizData.question: True or False: A simple, professional-looking static website with an SSL certificate is sufficient for a fake company's online presence.
quizData.explanation: True. It doesn't need to be complex. It just needs to exist and look legitimate on first glance.

slides.title: Email and Phone Infrastructure 📧📞
slides.content:
# Making Contact Seamless

**Email:**
- **DO NOT** use `david.chen.consulting@gmail.com`.
- **DO** use your fake domain: `david.chen@apex-solutions.com`.
- Set up **Google Workspace** or **Microsoft 365** for your domain. This gives you a professional email interface and calendar.
- **SPF/DKIM/DMARC:** Configure these to ensure emails don't go to spam.

**Phone:**
- Use a **VoIP service** (Google Voice, Burner, Twilio) with a number in the **appropriate area code**.
- **Voicemail:** Record a professional greeting: *"You've reached David Chen at Apex Solutions. Please leave a message."*
- **Call Forwarding:** Forward the VoIP number to your **burner phone** (or a softphone app) so you can answer live.

**Business Cards (In-Person Engagements):**
- Print 10-20 cards at a local print shop or online (Vistaprint).
- Include name, title, email, phone, and fake company logo.
- Handing over a **tangible card** is a powerful trust signal.

slides.title: DNS Records for Email Deliverability 📡
slides.content: Example SPF/DKIM/DMARC records to ensure your fake domain passes email authentication:
slides.codeSnippet:
; SPF Record (TXT)
v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all

; DKIM (provided by Google Workspace/M365)
; Add the generated DKIM key as a TXT record

; DMARC Record (TXT)
_dmarc.apex-solutions.com. IN TXT "v=DMARC1; p=none; rua=mailto:postmaster@apex-solutions.com"

slides.title: OPSEC for Digital Artifacts 🕵️
slides.content:
## Don't Cross the Streams

**Critical Rules:**
- **NEVER** log into your fake persona accounts from your **real IP address**. Use a **VPN** or a dedicated **cellular hotspot**.
- **NEVER** use the same browser profile for real and fake identities. Use **separate browsers** (e.g., Firefox for fake persona, Chrome for real) or **browser profiles**.
- **NEVER** pay for domain registration or services with a **personal credit card**. Use **prepaid debit cards** purchased with cash, or **cryptocurrency**.
- **ALWAYS** use a **password manager** (Bitwarden, KeePass) to keep fake persona credentials separate and secure.

**The 'Oops' Moment:** If you accidentally send an email from your real account to the target, **the engagement is over**. You've burned your identity and possibly exposed the Red Team.
